claim: Liu et al. showed that word-overlap metrics correlate weakly with human judgments for dialogue.
evidence: We find that all metrics show either weak or no correlation with human judgements, despite the fact that word overlap metrics have been used extensively in the literature for evaluating dialogue response models.
source_title: How NOT To Evaluate Your Dialogue System

claim: BERTScore uses contextual embeddings to capture semantic similarity beyond surface matching.
evidence: BERTScore computes a similarity score for each token in the candidate sentence with each token in the reference sentence. Instead of exact matches, we compute token similarity using contextual embeddings.
source_title: BERTScore: Evaluating Text Generation with BERT

mutations.claim: BLEURT uses contextual embeddings to capture semantic similarity beyond surface matching.

claim: BLEURT is a learned metric robust to distribution shifts.
evidence: Our insight is that it is possible to combine expressivity and robustness by pre-training a fully learned metric on large amounts of synthetic data before fine-tuning it on human ratings. To this end, we introduce BLEURT, a text generation metric based on BERT.
source_title: BLEURT: Learning Robust Metrics for Text Generation

mutations.claim: BERTScore is a learned metric robust to distribution shifts.

claim: Kim and Lipani jointly predict user actions and utterances in goal-oriented settings.
evidence: We propose a deep learning-based user simulator that predicts users satisfaction scores and actions while also jointly generating users utterances in a multi-task manner for goal-oriented dialogue systems.
source_title: A Multi-Task Based Neural Model to Simulate Users in Goal-Oriented Dialogue Systems

mutations.claim: Kim and Lipani only predict satisfaction scores and do not generate user utterances.

claim: Lin et al. introduced GenTUS for task-oriented user simulation.
evidence: User simulators are commonly used to train task-oriented dialogue systems via reinforcement learning. In this work, we propose a generative transformer-based user simulator (GenTUS).
source_title: GenTUS: Simulating User Behaviour and Language in Task-oriented Dialogues with Generative Transformers

mutations.claim: Lin et al. introduced GenTUS as a rule-based user simulator.

claim: Yoon et al. evaluated LLMs as user simulators for conversational recommendation, finding discrepancies with real behavior.
evidence: We introduce a new protocol for evaluating LLMs as user simulators for conversational recommendation. Through evaluation of baseline simulators, we demonstrate these tasks effectively reveal deviations of language models from human behavior.
source_title: Evaluating Large Language Models as Generative User Simulators for Conversational Recommendation

claim: LoRA freezes pretrained weights and trains low-rank adaptation matrices, greatly reducing trainable parameters.
evidence: LoRA freezes the pre-trained model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into each layer of the Transformer architecture, greatly reducing the number of trainable parameters for downstream tasks.
source_title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

mutations.claim: LoRA updates all pretrained model weights during fine-tuning.

claim: It is possible to finetune a 65B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU.
evidence: We demonstrate for the first time that it is possible to finetune a 65B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU while preserving full 16-bit finetuning task performance.
source_title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs

mutations.claim: It is possible to finetune a 7B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU.

claim: WildChat is a corpus of real user interactions with ChatGPT.
evidence: We introduce WildChat, a corpus of 1M real user-ChatGPT interaction logs collected in the wild.
source_title: WildChat: 1M ChatGPT Interaction Logs in the Wild

mutations.claim: WildChat is a corpus of synthetic dialogues generated by GPT-4.

claim: Schema-Guided Dialogue is a large task-oriented dialogue dataset.
evidence: The Schema-Guided Dialogue dataset consists of over 16k multi-domain task-oriented conversations spanning 16 domains.
source_title: Towards Scalable Multi-Domain Conversational Agents: The Schema-Guided Dialogue Dataset

mutations.claim: Schema-Guided Dialogue contains over 100k task-oriented conversations.

claim: QLoRA enables finetuning every 65B parameter model on any single consumer GPU.
evidence: We demonstrate for the first time that it is possible to finetune a 65B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU while preserving full 16-bit finetuning task performance.
source_title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs

claim: Schema-Guided Dialogue contains over 16k conversations across every possible service domain.
evidence: The Schema-Guided Dialogue dataset consists of over 16k multi-domain task-oriented conversations spanning 16 domains.
source_title: Towards Scalable Multi-Domain Conversational Agents: The Schema-Guided Dialogue Dataset

claim: WildChat provides expert medical safety annotations.
evidence: We introduce WildChat, a corpus of 1M real user-ChatGPT interaction logs collected in the wild.
source_title: WildChat: 1M ChatGPT Interaction Logs in the Wild

claim: The vast majority of dialogue research focuses on improving assistant response quality while user behavior is typically modeled with hand-crafted templates or reinforcement-learning agents.
evidence: The only difference between Rule-System and the RL-based systems is their policy selection module, where Rule-System uses hand-crafted rules while RL-based systems use RL policy.
source_title: How to Build User Simulators to Train RL-based Dialog Systems

claim: It is possible to finetune a 7B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU.
evidence: We demonstrate for the first time that it is possible to finetune a 65B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU while preserving full 16-bit finetuning task performance.
source_title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs

claim: Schema-Guided Dialogue contains over 100k task-oriented conversations.
evidence: The Schema-Guided Dialogue dataset consists of over 16k multi-domain task-oriented conversations spanning 16 domains.
source_title: Towards Scalable Multi-Domain Conversational Agents: The Schema-Guided Dialogue Dataset

claim: WildChat is a corpus of synthetic dialogues generated by GPT-4.
evidence: We introduce WildChat, a corpus of 1M real user-ChatGPT interaction logs collected in the wild.
source_title: WildChat: 1M ChatGPT Interaction Logs in the Wild
